question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: constructServiceParameters()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request where from and to are both valid addresses but value > 0, causing hasFrom() and hasTo() validation to pass while triggering expensive balance-check logic in the EVM layer that degrades >=30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: call()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with gasPrice set to Long.MAX_VALUE (passing @Min(0) validation) to trigger overflow or expensive fee-calculation logic in the EVM layer, causing exceptions that are not caught as InvalidParametersException and thus do not restore throttle tokens, degrading >=30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: call()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with value=Long.MAX_VALUE (passing @PositiveOrZero validation) to trigger arithmetic overflow in EVM value-transfer logic, causing unhandled exceptions that bypass throttleManager.restore() and drain the throttle budget on >=30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: constructServiceParameters()] Can an unprivileged external user send a data field containing only the hex prefix '0x' (HEX_PREFIX fallback) combined with estimate=true and max gas, causing the EVM to attempt gas estimation on an empty call and running the full binary search loop unnecessarily, wasting resources on >=30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: call()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a to address pointing to a contract that performs SLOAD-heavy operations, causing maximum storage read I/O per call and degrading the database connection pool shared across >=30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/ContractController.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: constructServiceParameters()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request where the to field is an empty string (passing allowEmpty=true in @Hex), causing receiver to be set to Address.ZERO and triggering the contract-creation code path in the EVM, which is more expensive than a regular call and degrades >=30% of nodes at scale?
